title: Research Report

prerequisites.text: Refer to the programme and departmental sections in the 2026 Health Sciences Faculty Handbook.

outline: Learning outcomes: By the end of this module, the student will be able to (i) critically review the research literature; (ii) conduct methodologically soun d and ethical research; (iii) analyse data and write up research results; (iv) discuss and critically evaluate research findings; (v) write up and present research. Content: Research literature on relevant topics; Application of research methodology; Data collection and analyses; Academic write-up and discussion of research findings; Research ethics. Skills: Students develop skills relate d to: teamwork; critique of th e literature; critical thinking; problem solving; interpretation of data; succinct reporting; academic writing; referencing; constructive engagement with feedback; dissemination of research findings. Teaching and learning activities: Students collaborate in small gr oups with the guidance of a research supervisor to review the research literature, conduct research; and analyse, report, discuss and present findings. Themes underpinning the course : Primary healthcare and contextual relevance; disab ility and burden of disease; ethics and human rights; bio-psycho-social models of health; developing agents for change; equity and affirmation of diversity.
lecture_times: See module block schedule and official class timetable.
dp_requirements: Attendance of all academic activities is required. Students are required to submit all coursework by the due date.
assessment: The final mark for the resear ch report consists of a gr oup project mark [80%] and a group presentation mark [20%] – each of which ar e weighted by an individual peer review mark. Students will need to obtain a minimum of 50% for their weighted research report mark and for their weighted presentation mark. If a student does not meet these requirements, they will need to engage with a supplementary assessment process.
